title: Mellow Malty Argentinian Chuck Roast Roast
description: A light, one-pan Argentinian dish featuring a tender chuck roast, served solo for a satisfying dinner. This recipe is Jain-friendly, using a comal to achieve a malty flavor without added sugars.
instructions: Season the chuck roast with salt, black pepper, and 1 tsp ground cumin, rubbing the spices evenly into the meat.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large comal-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the chuck roast until a nice crust forms, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to a preheated oven and roast the chuck roast at 350F (180C) for 2-3 hours, or until tender.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the roast rest for 20 minutes before slicing.
Transfer the sliced roast to a large plate and drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, then sprinkle with chopped fresh cilantro (optional).
